response.justification: The candidate has a solid 20-year track record as a hands-on full-stack and systems engineer, with genuine breadth across web, embedded, high-throughput services, and most recently AI-agent tooling. However, the Principal Engineer (AI Strategy) role is a director-level IC position requiring deep, demonstrated AI/ML expertise, recognized thought leadership in AI, and a track record of advising strategic customers at executive level on cloud and AI adoption — none of which are evidenced in the resume. The candidate's AI exposure is limited to a single current role working with MCP frameworks, with no prior ML background, no advanced AI degree, no cloud platform experience at scale, and no history of influencing organizational or customer strategy at the level this role demands.
strengths: 20+ years of continuous engineering experience across diverse stacks and domains, including high-throughput distributed systems (millions of req/s) and embedded systems, demonstrating genuine technical depth
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective and author of multiple open-source libraries, showing initiative and ability to build and ship independently
Community engagement through conference talks (FOSS4G) and multi-day workshops on TDD, Selenium, and web internals, indicating some capacity to share knowledge externally
concerns: AI/ML expertise is shallow and very recent — limited to current-role MCP tooling with no prior machine learning, model development, or AI platform work; far short of the 'recognized expertise with demonstrated AI products' the role requires
No evidence of director-level or strategic influence: no executive advisory work, no cross-org technical vision-setting, and no history of shaping cloud or AI strategy for customers or partners

No cloud platform depth — AWS CDK usage in one role and Kubernetes for a side project do not constitute the cloud architecture and strategy expertise expected for a principal-level cloud/AI strategy role
